Fix gameday modals not appearing when buttons clicked

Modals were invisible because open functions removed the 'hidden' class
but never added the 'active' class required by CSS to set opacity/visibility.
This caused body scroll to lock (page appeared frozen) while modals stayed
invisible. All three modals (Lock In, Pro Highlights, Clip Analysis) fixed.

fix(personas): preserve persona state across edit→view mode transition

After Save, the switch from editedData to enhancedFormData caused a
flash because the old contact prop didn't have the new personas yet.
Now we set optimistic personas before clearing edit state, bridging
the gap until the Firestore refresh arrives.

commit.message:
Fix Railway deploy: warn instead of exit on missing env vars

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(docker): restore access for users upgrading from pre-v2.0.0 (v2.0.5)

v2.0.0 introduced a non-root arrmate user, but data files created by older
images (users.db, services.json, etc.) were left with root ownership. The
arrmate user could not write to them, causing the UI to show a first-time
setup prompt and all login/register attempts to fail with an internal error.

- entrypoint.sh: use chown -R so all files under /data are fixed, not just
  the directory itself
- docker-compose.yml + docker-compose.prod.yml: add arrmate-init service that
  runs a recursive chown before arrmate starts, covering older images where
  the entrypoint lacked the -R flag
- bump version to 2.0.5
- add v2.0.5 release notes to README

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: scanner 401 recovery - clear stale in-memory cache, read fresh blob tokens

When a warm Vercel instance has an expired token in memory, it now:
1. Clears the in-memory cache
2. Re-reads fresh tokens from Vercel Blob (may have been synced)
3. Only then falls back to refresh token flow

Also: blob store now uses allowOverwrite:true for token updates.

commit.message:
feat: frequency-domain PID recommendations + Wiener data quality scoring (Phase A4-A5) (#147)

- PIDRecommender: add tfMetrics parameter for bandwidth/phase-margin-based rules
  - Low phase margin → increase D (damping)
  - Low bandwidth → increase P (responsiveness)
  - TF-based overshoot → same D/P logic as step-based
  - All TF recommendations capped at medium confidence
- DataQualityScorer: add scoreWienerDataQuality() for mixed-flight data
  - Sub-scores: signal duration, sample rate, stick activity, axis coverage
  - Coherence-based axis coverage when available
- PIDAnalyzer.analyzeTransferFunction: pass TF metrics to recommender
- 20 new tests (12 recommender + 8 quality scorer)

commit.message:
Split editor remaining counter; rules search debounce; enrichment timeout

Split editor (#6):
- Add live 'remaining' counter replacing the plain total display: shows
  '$X remaining' (amber), '$X over' (red), or '✓ Balanced' (green)
- Add '↓ Fill Last' button that adjusts the last row's amount to exactly
  cover the unallocated or excess amount, making splits balance in one click
- Save button was already disabled when unbalanced; remaining counter makes
  the gap immediately obvious without needing a save attempt

Rules debounce + enrichment timeout (#7):
- Add debouncedSearch state with 350ms delay so the rules search API is
  only called after the user pauses typing, not on every keystroke
- Add startedAt timestamp to enrichment jobs; polling preserves it across
  server status updates
- Enrichment banner now shows elapsed seconds and 'Taking longer than usual'
  amber warning after 90s; background shifts to amber
- 'Hide' button always visible while running (dismisses the UI bar without
  cancelling the server-side job); relabelled 'Dismiss' when done/error

commit.message:
Deploy pipeline fixes: non-blocking deploys, auto-deploy, Doppler RBAC (#80)

* fix: derive database host from DATABASE_URL env var

DatabaseConfig.URL() hardcoded localhost:5432, but in k8s the shared
postgres is at factory-postgres:5432. Now parses the host from the
DATABASE_URL env var when available, falling back to localhost.

This fixes pipeline creation failing on migrate-up in k8s pods.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(deploy): decouple deploy loop and restore repo after deploy

- Deploy check-ins run in a separate goroutine (30s interval) so they're
  never blocked by long-running implementation pipeline stages
- After deploy completes, fails, or rolls back, reset repo to main branch
  to prevent detached HEAD from blocking git pull on pod restart

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(deploy): mount staging secrets for deploy agent

Deploy agents run make targets that pass STAGING_* env vars to Helm.
These vars come from .env.staging locally but weren't available in k8s.

- Create k8s secret from .env.staging, mount at /etc/factory/deploy-env
- Entrypoint sources all mounted secrets into .bashrc for tmux sessions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(deploy): detect idle via pane content for non-blocking sessions

Deploy sessions never call WaitIdle, so their DB state stays
"factory_send" forever. The deploy loop now checks pane content
for idle indicators (Worked for/Brewed for) when state is
factory_send, allowing proper advancement to the next stage.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(deploy): broaden idle detection patterns for Claude Code

Claude Code uses randomly rotating verbs in the idle summary line
(Worked/Brewed/Baked/Cooked/Crafted/Built/Forged/Mixed for Xm Ys).
Match all known patterns, shared between idle detection and outcome
determination.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(deploy): improve outcome detection and add deploy-level vars

- Check success signals before fail signals in determineDeployOutcome
  to prevent false negatives from benign "failed" mentions in notes
- Remove overly broad "error:" and "failed" fail signals that triggered
  on build log noise
- Add Vars field to DeployPipeline config so deploy-level vars are
  available to all stages (e.g. environment: staging)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(web): add deploy detail page with live session streaming

Add /deploys/{sha} detail page with SSE live session streaming,
deploy event history, and clickable SHA links from the deploys list.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(deploy): skip failure-only stages during normal advancement

nextDeployStageID was blindly advancing to the next stage in the list,
which caused rollback stages (only reachable via on_fail) to run after
smoke-test succeeded. Now collects all on_fail targets and skips them
during normal sequential advancement.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(deploy): auto-deploy from GitHub issues labeled deploy_staging

Poll registered repos for issues with the deploy_staging label. When
found, create a deploy for HEAD of main and track the trigger issue.
On completion/failure/rollback, close the issue with a status comment.

- Add CloseIssue to github.Client
- Add TriggerIssue field to DeployState/DeployCreateOpts
- Add pollDeployIssues to deploy check-in loop
- Close trigger issue at all terminal deploy states

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(deploy): grant factory SA access to DopplerSecret CRDs

The Doppler sub-chart creates DopplerSecret resources in the staging
namespace. The factory deployer ClusterRole needs permission to manage
these CRDs for Helm upgrades to succeed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(deploy): add DopplerSecret RBAC and operator install docs

- Grant factory-deployer ClusterRole access to secrets.doppler.com
  API group (DopplerSecret CRDs) so deploy agents can manage them
- Add doppler-operator.yaml documenting how the Doppler K8s operator
  was installed (Helm-managed, not in this manifest)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: lucas <lucas@MacBook-Pro.local>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implementa Strato 0 completo di NeuroScacchi 2.0

- positionSchema.js: schema e validazione posizioni
- positions.json: 25 posizioni tattiche (12 temi diversi)
- TrainingSession: scacchiera interattiva con feedback e hint progressivi

refactor: convert verified/emailVerified/flagged booleans to DateTime fields

- emailVerified → emailVerifiedAt (DateTime?)
- flagged → flaggedAt (DateTime?)

action happened, and null naturally represents false.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: migrate send/recv plugins and app to WAIF streaming (#144)

* fix: migrate send/recv plugins and app to WAIF streaming format

Completed the full WAIL→WAIF migration across the audio pipeline:
- Moved FrameAssembler to wail-audio for shared use by recv plugin & recorder
- Updated send plugin IPC to stream WAIF frames (tag 0x05) instead of full WAIL intervals
- Updated recv plugin to assemble WAIF frames and decode via FrameAssembler
- Fixed recorder.rs to decode incoming WAIF frames (was calling decode on wrong format)
- Fixed channel capacity bottleneck: audio_tx 64→1024, ipc_from_plugin_tx 64→1024
- Fixed frame loss bug: changed try_send→send().await in IPC reader tasks for backpressure
- Updated all IPC E2E tests to match production WAIF streaming flow
- Un-ignored 3 optional wail-net tests (now run automatically)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: update architecture and CLAUDE.md for WAIF streaming migration

Update wire format, IPC protocol, and audio flow docs to reflect the
WAIF streaming format. Remove outdated #[ignore] test instructions from
CLAUDE.md. Add changeset for the migration.

Fix UrgencyBanner layout on all qualify pages — add flex-direction: column to .main

The .main section was display:flex without flex-direction:column, causing
UrgencyBanner and the form container to render side by side instead of
stacking vertically. Fixed across all 5 privacy tort qualify pages.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: resolve HttpTestServer listener race in Swagger and OpenApi3 tests

The HttpTestServer.restart() method starts a background listener thread
that calls s.close()/s.shutdown() after done() is called. When restart()
is called again, the previous listener may still be cleaning up the
socket, causing "Connection refused" or "SOCKET-NOT-OPEN" errors.

Fix: add a listener_done Counter that done() waits on before returning,
ensuring the listener thread has fully completed cleanup before the
socket can be reused by the next restart() call.

as literal asterisks. Replaced with a lightweight inline renderer:

- Empty lines → small spacer gap
- No new dependencies needed
